headlines.title: WILSON'S MEXICAN POLICY UNCHANGED
headlines.subtitle: President Says Lind's Visit Developed No Additional Plans. Special Ambassador Will Return on Chester to Vera Cruz.
headlines.fullText:
GULFPORT, Miss. Jan. 4 .- President Wilson today announced that his conference with John Lind had, developed no change in the Dailythe Washington administrator move Mexico and that Be new plan for more In the situation autoren che con The President referred to the conference de med that Mr. been although Mir. Lind constantly had heet er a few hours had been deemed worth ==== was of telegraphle communication, with the added value of affording an exchange of viewpoints regarding the situation in Mexico.
President Wilson added that there had been no special occasion for Mr. Lind's visit-no advices, excitement or new questions.
when asked it He was more hopeful for a speedy settlement of the trouble. es Ut the situation had not changed. The President has held that the Huerta government is slowly being crushed, not only by the constitutionalist terres, but through financial isolation and that It inevitably must fall. He smiled when told of reports that Provisional President Huerta of some high officials of the Huerta government were on board the Chester and laughingly anid:
"Well, I didn't see them If they were The President said that while Me Lind was not exactly enjoying lis lone ing to stay there and was deeply interested in the situation. Mr. Wilson apparently attached little Importance to the Lind episode, pointing out that the proximity of his personal envoy co. incident with his own vacation on the Mississippi conat had been as much as anything else a motive for the confer- He fald emphasis on the fact that nothing specific, such as the raining et embargo on arina and ammundo had been discussed. He said hoher: ermoon and that Mr. Lind would remain there Indefinitely.

The interview between the President and Mr. Lind was comparatively brief, although the Chester had come Ala mies in order that it might held. It was an ummun! incident- the arrival of the Chester carrying on powerstage accordled all the possessing no official status. Addins interest to the situation was the fact that the President went out to sea to meet Mr. Lind.

headlines.title: EX-JUDGE FACING LUNACY COMPLAINT PREFERRED BY WIFE
headlines.fullText:
Former District Judge Mike E Smith was locked in the county jail on a charge of aggravated assault satur- Gay. Mire Annette Smilla, his wife. afterward filed a funk against Smith in the county court. hun un esen Patrolmen Allen and Cogdill Friday night. He wam locked in the city jail for the night.
Smith was Judge of the Seventeenth district court for two terms preceding Judge Swayne. He made an enviable record on the bench and is generally known as one of the best district Juilges Tarrant county ever had.
Mr. and Mrs. Smith have been marriedl twice, obtained a divoree some years ago but they later remarried.
Mrs. Smith walted in the courthouse until the lunacy complaint was pre- Bending stanton building. County Detective Blanton signed the aggravated assault com- Plaint
Mr. and Mrs. Smith live at 1331 Hemphill street,

headlines.title: Chicago Girl to Get $500,000 if She Weds in Year
headlines.fullText: Miss Charletta Hall, nineteen-year. old Chicago stenographer, who is to receive $500,000 by her uncie's will if she weds within a year.

Coleman started in a large number of real estate deals, building several houses. headlines.title: PLAN FOR WATERWORKS REPAIRS IS ANNOUNCED
headlines.subtitle: Blanke Would Hypothecate Revenues for Operating Debts Already Existing.
headlines.fullText:
Commissioner Blanke's dan to make waterworks machinery repairs without a bond Issue was announced in Sat- urdas's session of the city commission, when he asked permission to begin re- pairs to one of the big pumps in the Holly station. He announced that fie proposed to make the repairs by day labor, but that some of the bills might be over $100 for materials. es amount a commismerenda Total gerne cost for the pump board. Total Tapair per to vegas he estimates to be about $4,000 or $5.000. Blanke asked permission to go ahead with the work by hypothecation of wa- terworks revenues for operating ex- penses already in existence. The ques- flos was raised it such repairs could be classed as operation. Blanke said they were necessary to operation dur- ing the coming summer.
At the request of Finance Commis- sioner Smith, action was deferred until the matter can be lald before the bankers. The pump upon rer pairs are proposed is that which the report of Expert Burdicke showed to have an efficiency of less than 50 per cent in its present condition.

headlines.title: COMMISSIONERS STUDY INTERURBAN SPEED
headlines.subtitle: Officials Ride on Special Car and Come to Fort Worth for West- to For bok Lunch.
headlines.fullText: medel city commission visited Fort Worth Saturday, a demonstration of the vari- ous speeds being made on the Inter- urban line between Dallas and Fort Worth In Dallas the speed limit for cars Is seven miles an hour. Fort Worth has the limit at eighteen milies except in the downtown district where a reasonable apped as low as may be needed for Following the tests on the Interurban the party came on to Fort Worth and had lunch at the Westbrook.

headlines.title: FIRST HOG BANQUET HELD IN GRAYSON
headlines.subtitle: Motto of Association Is "A Brood Sow on Every Farm."
headlines.fullText: SHERMAN. Texas, Jan. 2 .- The firat annual hog banquet of the Gray- mon County Heg Raisers Association, which has for its motto A Brood Sow on Every Farta, was held here today with covers laid for 190 guests. The association aims to promote bet- ter hog raising. Among the speakers tolay were Thomas Frazer and C. C. French of Fort Worth.

headlines.title: GO TO CAPITAL FOR CATTLE RATE REFUND
headlines.subtitle: Cattle Raisers" Officials . Go to Washington on Katy Overcharge Casa.
headlines.fullText:
Sam Cowan, attorney for the Cat- tie Raisers' Association of Texas, will go to Washington Monday or Tuesday In connection with the rate case against the Missouri, Kansas & Texas railroad for the refund of overcharges. A. C. association, ale association. went to Washington late Friday to assist in the final check of the cialis
Judge .Cowan will go to Washington Apon the Immediate settle- and cattlemen involved in the mait. About $200,009 will be paid to Texas cattlemen on over charges in freight rates. The case has been in the courts for ten years.

headlines.title: GROUNDS TO BE OPEN TO AVIATION CROWDS
headlines.fullText:
Unlike other aviation meets that have been held here, where the crowd was kept in the grandstand and bleachers, the proomters of Sunday's meet will throw open the gates to the grounds so that everyone can thor- oughly examine the monster flying machines, and hear Aviator R. C. King lecture on flying,
All arrangements have been com- Dieted for the aviation flight at Mor- Hwn company has promised wood car service to and from the park, and the biggest crowd that ever witnessed an attraction of the kind is expected to
Three flights will be made in the course of the afternoon. The first will take place promptly at # o'clock, when King In his Benoist flyer will leave the baseball park, make a circle of the city and returning will alight practi- cally at the same spot from which he started,
Twice this program will be carried out, and then King will pick up a pas- senger and make a flight across country to start. Texas, where Mart, Hiraios with ble auschina standar. w machine sfonday. n aviation and has a fine record among birdmen of the country as a fearless flyer.

headlines.title: POLL TAX PAYMENTS TOTAL 3,100 EVEN.
headlines.fullText:
Poll tax payments passed the 2,000 mark late Friday and piled up a surplus of seventeen for wood measure. At the close et business Thursday night the to- tal was 2.862. One hundred and fifty-five pald Friday. Tax Collector Elliott's office was fairly busy Saturday morn- and a good rush was expected
Eighty-three were Issued Sat- urday morning, bringing the to- tal to 3.100.

headlines.title: EXPLORER TO U. S.
headlines.fullText:
NEW YORK, Jan. 2-That Bir Er- nest Shackleton intends to visit Ameri- en before starting on his Antarethe ex- pedition, was stated in cable advices from London today.
This visit, It Is said, was deelded tip. on by the explorer to permit him to discuss his proposed expedition Cents of ana tema the many Americans who have made application to join his party.
